content.problemStatement:
You are given an N×N board where cells are labeled from 1 to N² in a Boustrophedon style (snake pattern) starting from the bottom left (board[N-1][0]) and alternating direction each row. Each cell contains either -1 (normal square) or a number indicating the destination of a snake/ladder at that position.

On each turn:


From current square 'curr', you can move to any square 'next' where next ∈ [curr + 1, min(curr + 6, N²)]
If you land on a snake/ladder (board[r][c] ≠ -1), you must move to its destination (board[r][c])
You only take a snake/ladder once per dice roll (no chaining of snakes/ladders)
The game starts at square 1 and ends when you reach square N².


Input Format

First line contains TTT - the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN - the size of the board
Next NNN lines contain NNN space-separated integers representing the board configuration




Output Format

For each test case:

Print a single integer - minimum number of dice throws needed to reach N2N^2N2
If it's impossible to reach, print -1
constraints: board[i][j] is either -1 or in the range [1, N2N^2N2]
Each cell contains either -1 or a destination square number
Squares 1 and N2N^2N2 are not snake/ladder starting points

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The path takes 4 moves:

Start at square 1 (row 5, column 0)
Move to square 2, take ladder to square 15
Move to square 17, take snake to square 13
Move to square 14, take ladder to square 35
Move to square 36 (final destination)
